title: WrestleMania 42 roundtable: Most anticipated matches, bold predictions

title: Angels' Garret Anderson, All-Star MVP, dies at 53
content: Outfielder Garret Anderson, who played 15 of his 17 major league seasons with the Angels and is a member of the team's hall of fame, has died at the age of 53.

title: Source: Texans, Anderson agree to $150M deal
content: Texans and All-Pro defensive end Will Anderson Jr. have agreed to a historic three-year, $150 million extension to make him the highest-paid non-quarterback, a source told ESPN's Adam Schefter.

title: Sources: Falcons trade Orhorhoro for Jags' Smith
content: The Falcons are trading DT Ruke Orhorhoro to the Jaguars in exchange for DT Maason Smith, sources tell ESPN's Adam Schefter.

title: Run it back: Liberty sign Big 3 to long-term deals
content: Liberty announced Friday that forward Breanna Stewart, center Jonquel Jones and guard Sabrina Ionescu all have signed multiyear deals to return to the team.

title: Sources: Brissett not at Cards' offseason program
content: Cardinals incumbent starting quarterback Jacoby Brissett has not been in attendance for the start of the team's offseason program as he waits for a new deal, sources told NFL Network.

title: Source: Padres nearing sale for MLB-record $3.9B
content: The Padres are on the verge of being sold for a record price of $3.9 billion, a source confirmed to ESPN.

title: WR Flowers: Harbaugh's practices led to injuries
content: Ravens wideout Zay Flowers says "heavy" practices under former coach John Harbaugh were the reason Baltimore "had a lot of injuries" last season.

content: Sky-high ticket prices won't be the only thing emptying the wallets of soccer fans attending World Cup matches at some U.S. venues this spring.

content: An early setback and a positive test -- since cleared -- didn't define Baumgardner. Now, she's on the doorstep of leading the sport.

title: Allahabad HC orders FIR against Rahul Gandhi in alleged dual citizenship case
content: The complaint was initially filed before the special MP/MLA court in Rae Bareli. However, on the petitioner's request, the High Court transferred the case to Lucknow on December 17, 2025.

content: A State government committee noted in its October 2025 report that the existing model of disbursement of grants has not resulted in excellence in academics or better financial discipline

title: SC agrees to hear CBI’s plea against HC order acquitting 4 in custodial-death case
content: The case pertains to the death of Udayakumar (28), who was allegedly taken into police custody in Thiruvananthapuram in September 2005 and was subjected to custodial torture, leading to his death

title: Heat, humidity make life miserable in north Kerala
content: Kozhikode records peak temperature of 37.2°C on April 16; maximum temperatures in Kasaragod, Malappuram, and Kannur above seasonal average

title: JD(U) likely to elect new head of legislature party at Patna meet on April 20
content: Senior party leader and Deputy Chief Minister Vijay Kumar Chaudhary could be elected JD(U) Legislature Party leader

title: Income Tax officials search premises linked to Senthilbalaji’s aides in Karur
content: There searches are said to be a result of intelligence reports that gifts, meant for distribution to voters, were stored in these places

title: Sabarimala hearing: Should judges rise above their religious perceptions when examining matters of conscience, SC judge asks
content: Senior advocate Rajeev Dhavan says a judge cannot be identified with his personal religion when asked to examine a matter of faith and conscience

title: Constitution Amendment Bill, part of delimitation package, defeated
content: The Bill falls short of the two-thirds mark of 352 votes, with 298 in favour and 230 against in the House where 528 members were present at the time of voting

title: Nine people dead as van plunges off Valparai cliff in T.N.
content: The deceased were part of a 12-member group from Malappuram district; three shifted to hospital in Coimbatore with severe injuries; police say the van they travelled in fell from a hairpin bend

title: BMRCL’s electrical and mechanical staff submit representation over operational and administrative concerns
content: The representation notes that managers and assistant managers are frequently engaged in multiple meetings, reducing their availability for core operational responsibilities

title: United Opposition defeated bid to harm Constitution: Kharge
content: “This is a decisive setback for the Modi government’s attempt to redraw India’s political map under the guise of women’s reservation,” says CPI general secretary D. Raja

title: Chaturvedi’s grumbling over defeat of Women’s Bill’ flies in the face of her party’s stand
content: Shiv Sena UBT MP Sanjay Raut tells The Hindu that they opposed it because the ‘BJP draped a pink saree on the delimitation bill’

title: Stalin stands exposed after PM Modi’s assurance on delimitation: Edappadi Palaniswami
content: Pointing out that the share of Tamil Nadu in the Lok Sabha would go up from 7.18% to 7.23%, Mr. Palaniswami, in an interview with The Hindu, said that “this disproves what he [Mr. Stalin] has been saying.”

title: Nine killed as van carrying Malappuram schoolteachers plunges off Valparai cliff
content: Most of the passengers in the van were teachers of Government Lower Primary School, Pang, and their family members. The van reportedly fell from hairpin bend number 13 and tumbled down to hairpin bend number 9.

title: We know how to bring the debt under control, improve the finances of Tamil Nadu, and implement all the welfare schemes completely, says the AIADMK general secretary
content: On delimitation, the Union Minister for Home Affairs has clarified that not only Tamil Nadu but other States also will not be affected. I have also reiterated it. Tamil Nadu’s share will go up from 7.18% to 7.23%. This has disproved what Mr. Stalin has been saying: Edapaddi K. Palaniswami

title: BJP to take to streets over defeat of women’s quota Bill in Lok Sabha
content: Women MPs from NDA hold protest outside Parliament, carrying placards and raising slogans against Opposition following defeat of Constitution Amendment Bill on women’s reservation and Delimitation Bill.

title: South stood united, made its voice heard and democracy prevailed: T.N. CM Stalin
content: M.K. Stalin says he ‘never opposed’ delimitation and always sought ‘fairness’ in a process that is ‘thought through’ and not pushed for ‘political gain’; he says women will ‘see through’ the BJP’s ‘optics’

title: Opposition defeated an attack on the Constitution, says Rahul Gandhi
content: Congress leader assures Prime Minister Narendra Modi of Opposition’s ‘unconditional support’ if the government wants to bring the 2023 women’s reservation law into force

title: Watch: Constitution Amendment Bill, part of delimitation package, defeated
content: Major political and geopolitical developments lead today’s headlines. The Constitution Amendment Bill on women’s quota faces a setback in the Lok Sabha, while Sri Lanka’s Power and Energy Minister resigns amid controversy. Iran has reopened the Strait of Hormuz, easing concerns over global shipping, even as remarks by Tejasvi Surya on Telangana trigger a political storm. The Supreme Court also flags illegal sand mining in Madhya Pradesh. Stay tuned for the full breakdown.

title: Schemes offering cash incentive to women takes centre stage is Bengal poll campaign

title: U.S.-Iran tension will not deter Haj pilgrims, say officials
content: The first batch of Haj pilgrims this year will depart on April 18 from various embarkation points in India

title: Man held with suspected MDMA, ecstasy pills in Kochi
content: He allegedly supplied narcotic substances to the youths who were arrested from a star hotel at Kadavanthra recently

title: Eight men arrested in Maharashtra’s Amravati sexual exploitation case
content: While an SIT has been formed by the government to probe the case, some politicians have attempted to give it communal colour by terming it ‘love jihad’

title: TCS case: pregnant Nida Khan to move court for anticipatory bail; police quiz her spouse
content: Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis says: “TCS is a very serious incident; we are trying to get to the roots of it”

title: TCS Nashik case: Company initiates probe, says Nashik unit not shut down
content: TCS said Nida Khan who has been described as an HR manager in some reports was a process associate and did not have any leadership or recruitment-related responsibilities

title: No supply restrictions, says Kerala State Electricity Board
content: This time the demand is high. But we have made all the arrangements for supply from central stations and purchases through power exchanges, says KSEB chairperson

title: With PM and HM away, Chief Ministers of BJP-ruled States step up campaign for party in West Bengal
content: Chief Minister of Maharashtra and Assam targeted the Trinamool govt. over decline of industries in the State, while U.P. CM criticised law and order situation in West Bengal

title: Punjab Minister’s home searched by ED; CM condemns BJP’s ‘raid regime as murder of democracy’
content: The ED reportedly searched 13 premises in Gurgaon (Haryana), Chandigarh, Ludhiana, and Jalandhar (Punjab): all residences and offices linked to Sanjeev Arora and his close associates

title: India welcomes Israel-Lebanon ceasefire announced by President Trump
content: India favours any step that will end hostilities in the West Asian region, says Ministry of External Affairs spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal

title: Principle of one person, one vote, one value violated, says Shah in Lok Sabha
content: Home Minister Amit Shah says “north-south divide” narrative is detrimental to the country and it will not diminish the government’s popularity; he says even if the Opposition “greyed their hair”, they will never come to power

title: March towards new India should not create a disunited India: Shashi Tharoor’s caution on delimitation
content: The Congress leader says there is near-unanimous support for women’s reservation and urged the government to pass it now to ensure it takes effect in the next general election on the existing seat matrix

title: Assembly Elections 2026 highlights: Abhishek Banerjee says BJP supporters abused TMC woman worker
content: Senior TMC leader hopes that the Election Commission will take action against those involved in the "heinous attack on the woman".

title: Tipra Motha scores massive win in TTAADC polls; BJP suffers defeat
content: The regional party founded and led by royal scion Pradyot Kishore Manikya has won 24 out of 28 seats, while BJP got four

title: Bihar CM Samrat Choudhary launches self-enumeration phase of Census 2027
content: Urges citizens to participate wholeheartedly in this national campaign and contribute towards conducting an accurate, inclusive, and reliable census

title: Rahul Gandhi’s ‘PM and I don’t have wife issues’ remark sparks laughter in Lok Sabha
content: Taking part in a debate on Bills related to the women’s reservation law and delimitation, Mr. Gandhi said members across the House had been shaped by women

title: Soaring consumption: Kerala State Electricity Board proposes TAM option, seeks regulatory commission’s nod
content: The power utility has also proposed an institutional mechanism comprising an apex committee and an operational committee for handling short-term power procurement during contingencies

title: IPL 2026: Shubman Gill shines as GT complete clinical chase, KKR remain winless
content: Gujarat Titans secured a five-wicket victory against Kolkata Knight Riders. Shubman Gill played a key role with an unbeaten 86. Earlier, Kagiso Rabada and Mohammed Siraj's bowling restricted KKR. Cameron Green fought back with 79 runs for KKR. Gill's innings guided Gujarat Titans to the target with ease.

title: Nvidia CEO won't accept losing China market: 'You are not talking to somebody who woke up a loser'
content: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang rejected the premise that the company would inevitably lose the Chinese market to domestic rivals like Huawei, calling the idea defeatist. Speaking on the Dwarkesh Podcast, Huang argued that computing ecosystems—unlike consumer products—are deeply sticky and hard to replace, pointing to CUDA's developer lock-in as a structural advantage. He also said US export controls have backfired, accelerating Chinese alternatives rather than slowing them down.

title: Jharkhand JAC Class 9 result 2026 released: Direct link to download scorecards
content: The Jharkhand Academic Council has declared the JAC Class 9 result 2026, with an overall pass percentage of 99.17%. Over 4.06 lakh students cleared the exam, with girls slightly outperforming boys. Most districts recorded above 98% results, reflecting strong performance. Students can check scores online or via SMS using their roll details.

title: US military may have a ‘dependence problem', and it may be linked to Elon Musk’s SpaceX
content: US military's growing reliance on SpaceX's Starlink network is raising operational concerns following recent disruptions. A Navy test in August 2025 caused a global Starlink outage, impacting unmanned ship visibility and highlighting risks of depending on a single provider for critical services.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO day 30: Ranveer Singh film eyes 'Pushpa 2's collection
content: Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge, directed by Aditya Dhar and starring Ranveer Singh, Sanjay Dutt, Arjun Rampal, R. Madhavan, Rakesh Bedi, and Sara Arjun, has made box office history by crossing Rs. 1,100 crore in India net collections, the first Hindi film ever to achieve this milestone. On Day 30, the film's cumulative India net stands at Rs. 1,103.51 crore, with a worldwide gross of Rs. 1,737.74 crore across 4,64,059 shows globally. Week 1 delivered a record Rs. 674.17 crore, including a Rs. 90 crore opening day, the biggest in Hindi film history. Stay updated with live Day 30 tracking, all weekly breakdowns, and the complete story of the film that rewrote Hindi cinema's box office rulebook.

title: IPL Points Table: Gujarat Titans surge into top four as KKR stay bottom
content: Gujarat Titans continued their strong IPL 2026 run with a five-wicket win against Kolkata Knight Riders. This marks their third consecutive victory, lifting them to fourth in the standings. KKR remain winless and at the bottom. Punjab Kings lead the table, followed by Royal Challengers Bengaluru and Rajasthan Royals. The mid-table is competitive.

title: Microsoft’s & Apple’s coding tools will be ‘dead soon,': Creator of Antrophic's AI coding tool
content: Boris Cherny, creator of Claude Code, predicts traditional coding tools like VS Code and Xcode will soon be obsolete. He believes AI agents will soon handle entire development workflows, rendering current IDEs unnecessary. This shift will redefine software engineers into 'builders' who orchestrate AI, requiring adaptability and early experimentation from professionals.

title: Horoscope Today: Daily astrological predictions for April 18
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for April 18, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: 'India has seen it, INDIA has stopped it': Rahul after women quota bill fails in Lok Sabha
content: Opposition leader Rahul Gandhi accused the government of an "unconstitutional trick" after the women's reservation bill failed in the Lok Sabha. He stated the INDIA bloc stopped the bill, which they viewed as an attempt to alter India's electoral structure, not a women's bill. The Congress urged the government to implement 33% reservation for women in the 2029 elections.

title: IPL 2026: KL Rahul’s homecoming meets RCB’s Chinnaswamy dominance
content: KL Rahul returns to his familiar M Chinnaswamy Stadium, aiming to regain form against his former team, RCB.  Delhi Capitals face a formidable RCB unit, strong at home, and need significant improvements in both their bowling and batting to compete. RCB, led by a resurgent Rajat Patidar, are in dominant form.

title: Dianna Russini photo drama: Husband involved in alleged Mike Vrabel affair rumorus
content: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach, and the former New York Times’ reporter and insider, Dianna Russini, continue to be a topic of discussion as their alleged affair rumors swirl now. While Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini have denied affair rumors, things have gotten worse as the popular NFL insider stepped down from her job at The Athletic. Amid this, there has been a new allegation against them.

title: NFL draft: Commanders linked to Jayden Daniels and four top college stars
content: Washington Commanders hold the 7th overall pick in the 2026 NFL Draft. They are reportedly inclined towards drafting than trading. Analysts link Ohio State safety Caleb Downs and WR Carnell Tate, along with LB Sonny Styles and Notre Dame RB Jeremiyah Love to the Commanders. Carnell Tate’s recent comments suggest a strong connection with veteran WR Terry McLaurin, hinting at a potential pick.

title: Carlos Alcaraz skips Madrid Open over injury, addresses online hate
content: Carlos Alcaraz has withdrawn from the Madrid Open due to a wrist injury, calling the decision painful as the event is close to his heart. He shared an emotional message with fans and admitted online criticism has affected him. After recent losses and physical struggles, his next target is a comeback at the Rome Masters.

title: When Vijay came knocking on voters’ doors in Perambur
content: The campaign was held at Jawahar Street in Bharathi Nagar, located next to the party’s election office.

title: Leo Tolstoy quote: 'Change comes from conscience, not resolution'
content: Leo Tolstoy, renowned author and philosopher, believed genuine life changes stem from an inner moral imperative, not mere mental resolutions. His quote emphasizes that true transformation arises when one can no longer live against their conscience, finding purpose and meaning in moral authenticity. This inner voice guides us towards an honest life.

title: AI-Powered Loyal Wingman Jets: HAL’s CATS to lead new combat era; UCAVs take high-risk missions
content: The future of aerial combat is shifting towards Collaborative Combat Aircraft (CCA), also known as loyal wingmen. These AI-equipped, unmanned drones will fly alongside manned fighters, enhancing their combat effectiveness and survivability. CCAs can perform diverse roles like reconnaissance, electronic warfare, and even strike missions, all without risking pilot lives.

title: Streamer 'xQc' leaves stream abruptly after falling sick
content: Felix xQc Lengyel ended his Twitch stream early after falling sick during training for the Sidemen Charity Match 2026. He admitted to vomiting and feeling unwell, while Nicholas Jynxzi tried to help him recover. During the stream, xQc also revealed he barely drinks water, raising concerns about his health ahead of the upcoming match.

title: 'This victory is just trailer': Stalin after Constitution Amendment Bill fails in LS
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister MK Stalin celebrated the defeat of the Constitution Amendment Bill in the Lok Sabha, calling it a victory for the state. He stated that Tamil Nadu fought for fairness in the delimitation process, not against it, and vowed to continue standing united against challenges to the state's rights and federal spirit.

title: IPL 2026: Impact role again on the cards for Virat Kohli?
content: Virat Kohli, nursing knee and ankle issues, surprisingly started as a reserve against Lucknow Super Giants. He then made a significant impact as an Impact Player, scoring 49 off 34 balls. Teammate Josh Hazlewood confirmed Kohli will play against Delhi Capitals, potentially continuing as an Impact Player due to his injuries.

title: Poll inducement seizures cross Rs 865 crore in West Bengal, Tamil Nadu ahead of elections
content: West Bengal and Tamil Nadu have collectively seen potential poll inducements worth over Rs 865 crore seized since February 26, 2026. West Bengal leads in liquor seizures, while Tamil Nadu reports the highest value of illegal cash confiscations. Both states are under strict directives from the Election Commission to ensure free and fair elections.

title: Former Angels star Garret Anderson dies at 53 after sudden medical emergency at home
content: Garret Anderson, former Los Angeles Angels star and franchise hits leader, has died at 53 after a medical emergency in Newport Beach. A key part of the Angels’ 2002 World Series team, he built a strong MLB career with consistent performances. Anderson later worked as an analyst and was inducted into the Angels Hall of Fame in 2016.

title: 'Ready for business': Trump says Hormuz open but US blockade on Iran continues
content: Iran has announced the Strait of Hormuz is fully open for commercial vessels. This decision is linked to a ceasefire in Lebanon. The passage will be open for the remaining truce period on a coordinated route. Previously, Iran had restricted passage to certain vessels. A separate US-Iran ceasefire is also nearing its end.

title: ‘He gets drunk, crosses limits’: Sumit alleges harassment by coach; Neeraj demands action
content: Paralympic medallist Sumit Antil has accused javelin coach Naval Singh of severe mental harassment and abusive behaviour, including drunken tirades targeting athletes and their families.  Olympic champion Neeraj Chopra has backed the complaint filed with the Sports Authority of India, demanding strict action against the coach for his "disgraceful" conduct.

title: Adult or minor? MP cops reach Kochi as viral Kumbh Mela girl's inter-faith marriage sparks storm
content: Madhya Pradesh police arrived in Kochi to investigate a controversial marriage involving a viral influencer, amid claims she is a minor. The woman, asserting she is 18, sought protection from Kerala Police after her family alleged she was underage. The MP team's visit follows a National Commission for Scheduled Tribes inquiry that deemed the marriage illegal.

title: ‘He should learn from his sister’: Amit Shah targets Rahul Gandhi after 'magician' dig at PM Modi
content: During the debate on the women’s reservation bill,  Union Home Minister Amit Shah and Leader of Opposition Rahul Gandhi both referenced Priyanka Gandhi during the discussion. Rahul Gandhi praised his sister in his speech, while also making comments on the Prime Minister that drew protests from ruling party MPs. Amit Shah later responded, targeting Rahul Gandhi over his remarks. The bill ultimately failed to secure the required two-thirds majority.

title: 'Sad day': Priyanka Chaturvedi breaks party line over women bill defeat in Lok Sabha
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) leader Priyanka Chaturvedi expressed disappointment as the Women's Reservation Bill failed to pass the Lok Sabha, calling it a setback for aspiring women legislators. The proposed amendment, aiming for 2029 implementation, required a two-thirds majority but fell short. This contrasts with her party chief's call for immediate implementation.

title: 'No money is changing hands': Trump rejects $20 bn Iran 'nuclear dust' deal claim
content: Following Operation Epic Fury, Donald Trump announced significant breakthroughs, claiming Iran's nuclear program has been suspended and the Strait of Hormuz reopened. He stated the US will secure "nuclear dust" without financial exchange, asserting Iran's nuclear ambitions are shut down and a broader conflict-ending deal is nearing completion.

title: Gautam Adani overtakes Mukesh Ambani as Asia’s richest person; check global rankings
content: According to the latest wealth rankings, Adani’s net worth has reached $92.6 billion, placing him ahead of Ambani, whose fortune is estimated at $90.8 billion. This shift has positioned Adani at the top spot both in India and across Asia.

title: From Hormuz to household finance: How global oil shocks can filter into Indian homes
content: Global oil markets face turmoil due to the Iran-US conflict and Strait of Hormuz disruptions. India, heavily reliant on oil imports, feels the pinch through rising inflation and borrowing costs. The Reserve Bank of India monitors the situation, opting for stability over aggressive action. Households may experience a gradual tightening of budgets as costs rise and monetary easing is delayed.

title: Vipul Shah wins cheque bounce case linked to Salman Khan, Ajay Devgn starrer 'London Dreams'
content: Filmmaker Vipul Amrutlal Shah has won a 16-year-old cheque dishonour case related to his 2009 film London Dreams. Shah's company provided financial aid to complete the movie, but the repayment cheque bounced. The Andheri Metropolitan Magistrate Court convicted producers PJ Singh and Gita Bhalla Singh, ordering them to pay within 90 days or face nine months imprisonment.

title: 'We will not allow north-south divide': Amit Shah counters opposition on delimitation
content: Union Home Minister Amit Shah criticized the Opposition's stance on the Women's Reservation Bill and delimitation, accusing them of promoting a divisive North-South narrative. He asserted that delimitation would ensure fair representation for all states, including southern ones, and refuted claims of any loss in their parliamentary seats.

title: RR's Romi Bhinder fined for phone breach; BCCI calls response 'unsatisfactory'
content: Rajasthan Royals team manager Romi Bhinder has been fined Rs 1 lakh by the BCCI for breaching match-day regulations by using a mobile phone in the team dugout during an IPL fixture. The Anti-Corruption Unit found his response to a showcause notice unsatisfactory, leading to the penalty and a warning for the first-time offense.

title: Viral AI video: Rakesh Bedi’s ‘Dhurandhar 2’ scene stuns fans
content: Aditya Dhar's 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' is a box office sensation, with Rakesh Bedi's character, Jameel Jamali, a Pakistani politician revealed as a 45-year undercover Indian agent, captivating audiences. A viral AI clip imagines Jamali confessing his true identity to his daughter, leaving viewers stunned and questioning its authenticity. The film has achieved a historic Rs 1000 crore net in India.

title: 'Centre trying to bypass OBCs and Dalits’: What Rahul Gandhi said on Delimitation Bill - Top quotes
content: Rahul Gandhi attacked the Women's Reservation Bill, calling it a political maneuver to alter India's electoral map rather than empower women. He alleged the government aims to weaken the political voice of OBCs and Dalits through constituency restructuring, vowing opposition to this "anti-national" move.

title: Gabrielle Union gives heartfelt 4-word shoutout to Megan Thee Stallion’s show
content: Gabrielle Union’s short but powerful reaction to Megan Thee Stallion’s Broadway performance highlights the impact of the rapper’s transition into theater. Taking on a historic role in Moulin Rouge! The Musical, Megan has delivered high-energy performances while navigating intense physical demands. A recent health scare briefly interrupted her run, but her quick recovery and commitment to return underline her determination. Union’s praise captures the audience sentiment, as Megan’s Broadway journey blends risk, resilience, and standout stage presence.

title: Strait Of Hormuz "Completely Open". But Iran's Guards Impose Fresh Conditions
content: The statement mentions details that Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi did not clarify when he announced that the passage during the ceasefire was "completely open" for all commercial vessels.

title: "Mine Threat In Parts Of Hormuz Not Fully Understood": US Navy Advisory
content: The TSS refers to the so-called Traffic Separation Scheme, which was adopted by the UN's shipping agency in 1968 with agreement of countries in the region.

title: Parliament Special Session Highlights: Constitutional Amendment Fails In Lok Sabha, Falls Short Of Two-Thirds Majority
content: Parliament's 3-day special session Live Updates, Day 1: The government is expected to introduce three amendment Bills aimed at operationalising the legislation, which was passed in 2023 to provide 33% reservation for women in the Lok Sabha and State

title: 9 Tourists Killed As Van Falls In Gorge Into Tamil Nadu, PM Condoles Deaths
content: The van carrying 13 people from Kerala's Malappuram district lost control while descending the Valparai-Pollachi hill road.

title: Rain, Gusty Winds Hit Delhi As Met Department Warns Of Thunderstorms
content: Delhi saw an unusual spell of rain on Friday leading to a drop of 7-10 degrees Celsius in temperatures.

title: Liquor, Goat Liver, Aghori Rituals: Self-Styled Godman's Web Of Lies Exposed
content: Accused Rhythm Ashok Panchal alias Monty, on the basis of such fake claims, lured those seeking solutions to personal and professional problems.

title: Petrol Queues, Search Spikes: India's Oil Panic Eases After Hormuz Opens
content: Diesel demand jumped to 8.72 million tonnes, up sharply from 7.66 million tonnes in February, while petrol consumption surged to 3.77 million tonnes.

title: Indian Navy Veteran Can't Return From Qatar Despite Commuted Death Sentence
content: Purnendu Tiwari was among eight former Navy personnel arrested by Qatari authorities on espionage charges in 2022.

title: Trump Says US, Iran "Very Close" To Having Peace Deal
content: Trump's comments came after a series of social media posts in which he touted progress on reopening the Strait of Hormuz and ending Iran's nuclear program.

title: Trump Says US And Iran Are Working Together To Clear Mines From Hormuz Strait
content: Last week, the New York Times reported that Iran was unable to fully reopen the Strait of Hormuz because it could not locate all the mines it laid in the waterway to block the passage amid war.

title: World Hemophilia day: The bleed that never really stops
content: hemophilia is a disorder of clotting . the body is missing or low on specific proteins known as Factor VIII or Factor IX, which tell blood to stop flowing after an injury . without them, even internal bleeding into joints or muscles can become life-thrill .

title: 80-year-old Sikh, inspired by Fauja Singh, to run London Marathon for first time
content: an 80-year sikh man is running the London Marathon for the first time on April 26 . he was inspired by Fauja Singh who encouraged him to take up walking initially . the clubhouse was built in Fausja's memory in redbridge, on the route .

title: A passage through Hormuz: US and Iran signal end to crisis

title: US plans N-reactors in space orbit by 2028, on moon in 2030

title: Transfer rumors, news: Arsenal lead race for Newcastle's Anthony Gordon
